Material.fragment: fashioned from dull pewter worn grey by many hands
insults: Your answer was as dull and lifeless as pewter left in the rain!
Even a pewter-brained simpleton could have done better!
Pewter! That is the worth of your so-called intellect — dull, common, unremarkable!
You have the mental acuity of a pewter spoon, and I am being generous!
A pewter mind in a golden age — how tragically fitting!
compliments: Tolerable, I suppose. Even pewter has its uses — though not many.
Correct. Like pewter, your answer is functional if utterly lacking in elegance.
You stumbled upon the answer like a pewter coin in the mud — by accident.
I grant you this one. A pewter victory, dull but technically valid.
Inconceivable that you got it right. As surprising as pewter turning to gold.
hints: Sometimes what endures is not the brightest, but the most resilient.
Think of what is common yet indispensable — not precious, but present.
The answer is humble, like a craftsman's work rather than a king's treasure.
It is not silver. It is not gold. But it has served for centuries.
What is grey, cold to the touch, and found in every tavern in the land?

Material.fragment: wrought from polished silver that catches the light like a secret
insults: Your answer had the silver-tongued charm of a roadside swindler — none!
Silver linings are wasted on someone with your leaden thinking!
Even a silver-plated excuse would serve you better than that answer!
As hollow as a silver bell cracked down the middle — that is your intellect!
Silver-spoon upbringing, silver-nothing brains!
compliments: A silver answer to a golden question — acceptable, if underwhelming.
Correct. Even a silver spoon occasionally finds good broth.
You answered as if by silver instinct rather than actual intelligence.
I concede the point. Like silver, your answer was at least worth something.
The silver lining of your otherwise catastrophic reasoning — you got it right.
hints: What shines brightest in moonlight and is associated with purity and worth?
Think of something valuable but not the most valuable — second only to gold.
It is the colour of starlight and worn by those who wish to impress.
Cold to the touch, lustrous, and found in the coffers of those with taste.
It reflects your face back at you and has been traded for kingdoms.

Material.fragment: hewn from dark oak that smells of old forests and older wars
insults: Your reasoning is as thick and unyielding as a knotted oak plank!
An oak-headed response — dense, immovable, utterly wrong!
Oaken stubbornness is your only virtue, and even that is misplaced!
You are as hollow inside as a rotted oak — impressive outwardly, empty within!
The oak knows not why it stands — much like you know not why you answered that!
compliments: Correct, though I suspect you are as surprised as I am. Solid as oak, by accident.
An oaken answer — reliable, if lacking in any sophistication whatsoever.
Even the oak breaks eventually. You have not broken yet — give it time.
Like oak, your answer stood firm. It will be the last time, I assure you.
I grudgingly concede. Oaken stubbornness occasionally produces correct results.
hints: Think of something ancient and enduring — something that grows slowly and lasts forever.
It comes from the forest, has been used for ships and castles alike.
What wood is most associated with strength, longevity, and the smell of old libraries?
It burns long and slow and provides the foundation for things meant to last.
Kings have sat upon thrones of this — and been buried in it too.

Material.fragment: carved from yellowed bone smooth as river stone and cold as winter
insults: Your answer chilled me to the bone — and not with respect!
Bone-dry of wit, bone-dry of sense — a remarkable achievement in emptiness!
Even the bones of the dead hold more wisdom than your response!
You have the hollow bravado of a bone without its marrow — impressive until examined!
A bone-headed answer in every sense of the word!
compliments: Correct. Even bones outlast their owners — as your answer has outlasted my expectations.
I am bone-weary of your occasional competence. Correct.
Like bone, your answer is stripped of everything unnecessary. Correct, if bleak.
You have answered correctly. I find this development as unsettling as bare bone.
The structural integrity of your answer surprises me. Like bone, it holds.
hints: Think of what remains when everything else has gone — the lasting, inescapable core.
It is pale, hard, and carries the memory of what it once was.
What do the dead leave behind that the living still use?
Cold, smooth, carved — found in places of great age and quiet.
It is the architecture beneath all things — unseen until the end.

Material.fragment: carved from obsidian black as a moonless night and sharp as spite
insults: Your answer was as dark and unreflective as obsidian — nothing shines through!
Obsidian is sharp! Your answer was not!
Black as obsidian is your reasoning — opaque, volcanic, and ultimately useless to me!
You shatter like obsidian under the slightest pressure of actual thought!
The obsidian darkness of your intellect is truly something to behold — and pity!
compliments: Obsidian-sharp, that answer. Against all expectation, correct.
As dark and surprising as obsidian catching light — you got it right.
Correct. Like obsidian, your answer had an unexpected edge to it.
The volcanic clarity of your answer astonishes me. Correct.
Sharp as obsidian — which is the only thing I can say in your favour.
hints: Think of something that comes from fire and pressure, forged in the earth's own violence.
It is black, volcanic, and sharper than any blade when broken right.
Ancient peoples made weapons of it — it is darkness given form and purpose.
What is made of glass but formed by no glassblower's breath?
It catches light unexpectedly — brilliant and dangerous in equal measure.

Rim.fragment: its rim gilded in flaking gold that was once magnificent
insults: Gilded foolishness is still foolishness — your answer proves it!
You dress up your ignorance like a gilded cage — beautiful from afar, wrong up close!
A gilded response with nothing genuine beneath the gold — typical!
Even gilded nonsense remains nonsense, and yours is the finest I have encountered!
You are all gilded surface and no substance whatsoever!
compliments: Correct. Even gilded accidents are still correct.
A gilded answer — correct, and entirely more attractive than it deserves to be.
You have gilded your reputation slightly with this answer. Do not get used to it.
Correct, though your gilded luck cannot hold forever.
Like gilded trim on a common cup — surprisingly pleasing. Correct.
hints: What suggests wealth and importance without necessarily possessing either?
Think of something decorated beyond its worth — the ornament, not the substance.
Gold applied to the surface of something — beautiful, perhaps dishonest.
It catches the eye and suggests value. The eye is easily fooled.
What do kings put on things to make them seem more important than they are?

Rim.fragment: its rim crusted with salt as though the sea itself had drunk from it
insults: Your answer leaves a salty taste — and not the good kind!
Salty ignorance, brined in years of not thinking — that is what you offer me!
As preserved in brine as your outdated notions — salted and useless!
You have rubbed salt into the wound of my patience with that answer!
A salty response from someone whose wit has been preserved past its prime!
compliments: Correct. Like salt, your answer was unexpected and curiously useful.
Worth its salt, that answer — which is the only compliment I will offer.
I am preserved in astonishment. Salted by surprise at your correctness.
Like salt in a wound — your correctness stings me. Well done.
A salty correctness, I will grant you that.
hints: Think of something that preserves, that seasons, that comes from the sea.
It is found on every table and at the edge of every ocean.
Ancient civilisations paid wages in this — it is worth more than it appears.
What leaves a white crust when seawater dries? What seasons the world?
It burns wounds and flavours feasts in equal measure.

Rim.fragment: its rim chipped and ragged as though it had survived several arguments
insults: Your answer is as chipped and broken as your capacity for rational thought!
Chipped — like your dignity after offering that response!
You are chipped at the edges and hollow at the centre — a perfect metaphor!
Like something chipped away at by incompetence — that is what you have done to my patience!
A chipped response! Cracked, incomplete, and showing its damage proudly!
compliments: Correct — and I feel somewhat chipped at by the surprise of it.
Chipped away at my certainty of your failure, you have. Correct.
Like something chipped and yet still functional — your answer holds. Correct.
Even chipped things serve their purpose. As, apparently, do you. Correct.
I am chipped at my patience but impressed despite myself. Correct.
hints: What shows the damage of age and hard use — worn, but still here?
Think of something that has been through much but persists despite its wounds.
It is imperfect, scarred by time and use, yet still serves its function.
The edges are broken but the whole remains. What endures despite damage?
What do things look like after a long journey and many years?

Rim.fragment: its rim engraved with symbols too small and strange to read
insults: Your answer might as well be engraved in gibberish — it means as much!
Engraved in the annals of terrible responses — that is where yours belongs!
You have engraved your incompetence on the memory of everyone present!
As elaborate and ultimately illegible as bad engraving — your answer!
Beautifully engraved nonsense is still nonsense, as you have demonstrated!
compliments: Correct. Engraved in my memory as the time you surprised me.
Like fine engraving, your answer is detailed and unexpectedly correct.
I shall have it engraved: you were right. Just this once.
Your answer has the precision of engraving. Correct.
Engraved correctness — rare, elaborate, and somehow genuine.
hints: Think of something decorated with purpose — words or patterns cut deliberately into it.
What bears marks meant to last — a message made permanent?
It carries a message in a language of lines and shapes.
Permanence is its hallmark — once carved, it cannot be uncarved.
What contains writing that was put there with great effort and intent?

Rim.fragment: its rim wide and flat as a country road welcoming every traveller
insults: Your answer was as wide of the mark as a wide-mouthed fool shouting into the wind!
Wide open spaces between your ears — that is where your answer came from!
Wide-eyed ignorance has never impressed me, and it does not start now!
Your reasoning is as wide and shallow as a summer puddle!
Wide-ranging in your wrongness — I will give you that much!
compliments: Wide of the wrong answer, which puts you squarely in the right. Correct.
A wide and generous correctness. I accept it grudgingly.
Correct — and it came to you with all the wide confidence of the truly lucky.
Wide open correctness, as surprising as a clear horizon.
You cast wide and caught the right answer. Beginner's luck.
hints: Think of something broad, open, and welcoming — that does not narrow or restrict.
What offers the most room? What is most open to possibilities?
It is expansive, unhurried, built for generosity rather than precision.
The answer is not hidden or narrow — it is broad and in plain sight.
What do you find at the top of something meant to be filled?

Handle.fragment: its handle encircled by a ring of tarnished copper
insults: Your answer runs in circles like a ringed fool who cannot find the exit!
Ringed — as in your reasoning goes round and round and arrives nowhere!
You are ringed by mediocrity and see no way out!
A ringed performance — circular, repetitive, and ultimately pointless!
Even a ringed simpleton on market day would have done better!
compliments: Correct. Even ringed fingers occasionally grasp the right thing.
You have ringed the correct answer like a finger finding its fit.
Ringed with unexpected accuracy — correct.
Like a well-fitted ring, your answer is exactly right. I resent it.
Correct — and it comes full circle despite your best efforts at failure.
hints: Think of something circular — going around, enclosing, binding without closing off.
What goes round and round and marks something important?
A circle is both a beginning and an ending — and this answer is much the same.
It connects two things without being either — a joining of parts.
What forms when something wraps around without a seam?

Handle.fragment: its handle cracked and broken at the joint, repaired with tar and stubbornness
insults: Your answer is as broken as your grip on logical thought!
Broken — much like my faith in your capacity to answer anything correctly!
You have broken the record for wrongness. Congratulations on this achievement!
A broken answer from a broken intellect — at least there is symmetry!
Broken things have their uses. Your answer, however, does not!
compliments: Correct — though I expected something more broken from you.
Like a mended crack, your answer holds despite its origins. Correct.
Broken of my certainty that you would fail, you have. Correct.
Even broken instruments occasionally strike the right note. Correct.
Correct. I am somewhat broken inside by this development.
hints: Think of something that has failed but been put back together — imperfect but functional.
What endures damage and continues? What is repaired rather than replaced?
It is not whole, but it serves. Fragile in places, persistent everywhere.
The fault line is visible, but the thing still stands. What does that describe?
What shows its history in its damage?

Handle.fragment: its handle set with small dull gems that were once considered precious
insults: Bejeweled nonsense is still nonsense — you have decorated your ignorance admirably!
You are bejeweled with wrong answers — quite the collection!
A bejeweled fool is still a fool — and you, sir, are the crown jewel!
Your response glistens with false promise like cheap bejeweled costume work!
Bejeweled incorrectness — you have at least made your failure attractive!
compliments: Bejeweled with correctness — I cannot dispute it, however much I wish to.
Your answer glistens with rare accuracy. Bejeweled and correct.
Correct — and like a bejeweled thing it is more impressive than expected.
I am bejeweled with admiration I do not wish to feel. Correct.
A bejeweled exception to your usual standard. Correct.
hints: Think of something decorated with gems — precious, ornate, built to impress.
What glitters with colour and catches every eye in the room?
It is adorned beyond necessity, yet the adornment is the point.
Small precious things set into something larger — the detail that changes everything.
What carries its wealth on its surface for all to see?

Handle.fragment: its handle carved into the likeness of a serpent devouring its own tail
insults: Your answer was carved from the wood of pure ignorance!
Carved in stone is your wrongness — immovable, permanent, undeniable!
You have carved a monument to mediocrity with that response!
As carefully carved as your response — which is to say, not at all!
A carved fool is still a fool, however decorative the craftsmanship!
compliments: Carved from the truth, that answer. Correct.
Like fine carving, your answer shows unexpected skill. Correct.
Correct — carved out of your otherwise blank canvas of thought.
I concede. As carved and deliberate as a master's work. Correct.
Your answer is carved into my memory as the exception. Correct.
hints: Think of something shaped by patient work — detail added by hand, with purpose.
What is made by removing what is unnecessary until only the truth remains?
It has a form given to it by craft — shaped by someone who knew what they wanted.
The artisan's answer — worked at slowly, precisely, with intention.
What is made by a craftsperson who started with one thing and ended with another?

Handle.fragment: its handle twisted as a lie told three times and believed on the fourth
insults: Your reasoning is as twisted as a wrung cloth — and about as useful!
A twisted logic leads to twisted answers, and you have provided both!
You have twisted the truth so far it has snapped entirely!
Twisted! That is the only word for your convoluted wrongness!
Even twisted minds occasionally find the right path — you have not!
compliments: Correct — though your path to the answer was as twisted as your usual reasoning.
Twisted around to correctness somehow. I grudgingly accept it.
Like a twisted path that leads home, you have arrived. Correct.
Correct — against all probability and through logic I cannot follow.
Twisted into rightness — it happens, I suppose. Correct.
hints: Think of something wound upon itself — shaped by turning, not by straightness.
What is formed by spinning or coiling — gaining strength through its complexity?
It is not straight. It is not simple. But it holds together despite that.
Rope. Vine. The road that takes the long way to be sure. What does this suggest?
What is stronger for being braided, and more beautiful for being irregular?

Base.fragment: sitting on a flat base as steady and unremarkable as common sense
insults: Your answer falls flat — as flat as the plainest floor in the dullest building!
Flat-footed wrongness — you stumbled over your own answer!
As flat and uninteresting as your response! Utterly one-dimensional!
A flat answer from a flat mind — geometry has never been so disappointing!
Flat as the world before men knew better — and wrong in the same direction!
compliments: Correct. Flat as the truth when there is nowhere left to hide.
A flat, sturdy correct answer. It holds.
Correct — and it lies flat on the table of fact where it belongs.
Flat correctness. Unglamorous, reliable, right.
As flat and dependable as bedrock. Correct.
hints: Think of something level, stable, and offering no surprises.
What provides the most reliable foundation — never tilting, never shifting?
It is the simplest form. No height. No depth. Just there.
What is at the very bottom, holding everything above it in place?
The answer that supports everything else — plain, solid, essential.

Base.fragment: its base cracked in a long spiderweb of a fracture barely held together
insults: Your argument is as cracked as a drought-split mud plain — and as empty!
Cracked! Your reasoning has cracked under the slightest pressure of examination!
The cracked foundation of your thinking has collapsed, as I expected!
A cracked intellect — fascinating in its fragility, useless in its purpose!
You have cracked the record for wrongness yet again!
compliments: Cracked me open with surprise, you have. Correct.
Correct — even through the cracked surface of your thinking, light got in.
Like something cracked but not broken, your answer holds. Correct.
The cracked surface hides structural integrity — correct against expectation.
I am cracked with surprise at this correctness. Well done.
hints: Think of something that shows its age and its journey in its damage.
What is still standing despite the visible fractures?
It is not perfect. It shows the record of what it has endured.
The cracks are a map of all the stresses that tried and failed to break it.
What is near to breaking, yet somehow still whole?

Base.fragment: its base inlaid with a pattern of pale stone set in dark mortar
insults: Your answer has all the depth of inlaid floor tile — decorative, flat, underfoot!
Inlaid into the very stone of failure — that is where your answer belongs!
As inlaid with false confidence as a floor that believes itself a ceiling!
You have inlaid wrong answers into the permanent record of this conversation!
Inlaid wrongness — decorative, at least, but still completely wrong!
compliments: Correct. Inlaid into the mosaic of your rare competence.
Like fine inlaid work, your answer is precise and unexpectedly beautiful. Correct.
Inlaid into the record of your achievements — small but real. Correct.
Correct. As intricate and deliberately placed as fine inlay work.
You have inlaid correctness where I expected only blankness. Well done.
hints: Think of something set into a surface — embedded, deliberate, part of the whole.
It is placed with precision in a larger context, inseparable from what surrounds it.
What is put into something else so carefully that it seems to have always been there?
A pattern within a pattern — the small thing that completes the large thing.
What is fitted into place with purpose, neither protruding nor receding?

Base.fragment: its base rough and unfinished as though the craftsman lost interest at the end
insults: Rough — as rough as your grasp of the obvious, which is to say, not at all!
A rough and unfinished answer — started badly and abandoned worse!
Rough around the edges and hollow in the middle — your answer in full!
You have roughed out an answer without ever refining it into correctness!
Rough work from a rough mind. I expected little and you delivered less!
compliments: Rough correctness is still correctness. Correct.
Like rough stone that holds a castle together — correct beneath the surface.
Correct — rough in arrival but sound at the core.
A rough-hewn correct answer. Workmanlike but right.
You have roughed your way to correctness. I cannot deny it.
hints: Think of something unpolished, unfinished, closer to nature than to craft.
What is strong without being pretty — functional without ceremony?
It is not refined. It does not pretend to be. And it is stronger for that.
The first form of something, before the work of smoothing begins.
What texture do things have before someone cares enough to change them?

Base.fragment: its base polished to a mirror-shine that reflects the table beneath it
insults: Polished ignorance — you have buffed your wrongness to a fine sheen!
As polished as your delivery and as empty as your answer!
You have polished your incompetence until it gleams — I will give you that!
Polished! The surface shines but the depths are hollow!
A polished fool — gleaming with confidence, empty of substance!
compliments: Polished correctness. It reflects well on you. Correct.
Correct — as clean and polished as it is unexpected.
Like a polished surface, your answer reveals more than it conceals. Correct.
I see myself in this correct answer, and I am polished into admitting it.
Polished to perfection — your answer is right, however improbable.
hints: Think of something refined to its finest state — worked until no roughness remains.
What has been improved by patient attention until it shines?
It reflects the world back at you, smooth and without distortion.
What is the result of removing everything imperfect?
When something is at its most complete form — smooth, bright, finished.

Condition.fragment: bearing the look of something ancient enough to have a legend attached
insults: Ancient! Your answer belongs in the time before thinking was invented!
As ancient and irrelevant as the error you have just committed!
You dredge up ancient wrongness as though age were an excuse!
Your answer is as ancient as bad ideas and just as persistent!
Ancient mediocrity — preserved through centuries by sheer stubbornness!
compliments: Correct. Ancient wisdom occasionally trickles down even to you.
Like something ancient, your answer has survived the test of examination. Correct.
Ancient and correct — I find this as unsettling as I find most old things.
Correct. The ancient ways sometimes work, and today is apparently one such day.
Ancient correctness. Time has proven it, and so have you.
hints: Think of something with deep history — older than memory, present before language.
What has been here since before anyone can remember, and will be here after?
It carries the weight of centuries and the dignity of endurance.
What do we call things that existed before the world was explained?
Older than the kingdom, older than the road — what has always been?

Condition.fragment: kept in pristine condition as though never once used for its purpose
insults: Pristine wrongness — you have kept your error in perfect, unmarked condition!
Your answer is pristine — completely untouched by correctness!
Pristine as the blank space where your answer should have been!
You have pristinely avoided the truth in every direction!
Pristine in its total wrongness — I almost admire the consistency!
compliments: Correct — pristine and exact and surprisingly untarnished by your usual method.
Pristine correctness. Not a flaw in it. Well done.
Like something pristine kept behind glass, your answer is perfect. Correct.
Correct. Pristine. I find I have nothing to complain about, which is its own problem.
Pristinely correct. Not a scratch on it.
hints: Think of something kept perfect — unmarked by use, untouched by time.
What is exactly as it was the day it was made — no wear, no age?
It has never been used for its purpose. It exists to be preserved.
Perfect condition speaks of care, wealth, and perhaps a reluctance to live in the world.
What looks as though it has just been made and never since touched?

Condition.fragment: tarnished to a warm brown that suggests decades of neglect and occasional use
insults: Tarnished! Your reputation for correct answers, already questionable, is further tarnished!
Your answer has tarnished whatever credibility remained to you!
As tarnished and dull as the thinking that produced that response!
Tarnished logic yields tarnished results — and you have yielded magnificently!
You have tarnished the fine tradition of attempting to answer correctly!
compliments: Correct — tarnished by surprise but correct nonetheless.
Like something tarnished that still holds its value, your answer does too. Correct.
Tarnished by the long road to get here, but correct upon arrival.
Correct. Even tarnished silver is still silver.
I concede. Tarnished but true.
hints: Think of something that has aged — not broken, but marked by time and weather.
What loses its surface brightness without losing its essential nature?
It was once brilliant and still has worth — changed by time but not ruined by it.
The patina of long years and hard use — what wears it honestly?
What shows its history on its surface in the form of colour and texture?

Condition.fragment: scorched along one side as though it survived something it should not have
insults: Scorched! Your answer scorches the very concept of logical thinking!
You have scorched the earth of this conversation with that response!
As scorched and ruined as your chances of being correct!
Scorched-earth wrongness — you have burned every bridge to the right answer!
That answer scorched my patience to the roots!
compliments: Scorched me with surprise, you have. Correct.
Correct — like something scorched but not destroyed.
You have scorched your way through to the right answer. Correct.
Scorched but standing — your answer survives. Correct.
Correct. It burns me to say so, but it is right.
hints: Think of something that has been through fire — marked by it, but not destroyed.
What carries the evidence of great heat upon its surface?
It survived something that should have ended it. The damage is the proof.
Blackened on one side, whole on the other — what does this image suggest?
What is changed by fire but not consumed by it?

Condition.fragment: so ornate as to suggest it was made to impress rather than to be used
insults: Ornate in its wrongness — you have decorated your failure magnificently!
As ornate and useless as an answer that looks right and isn't!
Ornate foolishness is the worst kind — it wastes everyone's time, including the fool's!
You have offered an ornate display with no substance — typical!
Ornate! Elaborate! Completely, magnificently wrong!
compliments: Ornate correctness. It has everything. Including being right. Correct.
Correct — ornate in its arrival but genuine in its content.
Like something ornate that is also functional — rare and correct.
Correct. I am ornately furious at having to say so.
Ornately correct. I cannot improve upon it, and I find that irritating.
hints: Think of something made with excessive decoration — beautiful for its own sake.
What is built not for use but for impression? For ceremony rather than function?
It is more than it needs to be, and it knows this, and it doesn't care.
What is created to declare importance rather than to serve it?
Elaborate beyond necessity — what fits this description?
